seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: change the goal from a plain same-channel conflict count to a weighted distance-constrained labeling (T-coloring), where each contact pairing has its own separation demand d and residual risk w*max(0,d-|c_u-c_v|); and generalize the input from a clean transmitter graph to an over-constrained pandemic contact network (K+1 cliques + diffuse community edges) so no perfect assignment exists and greedy vs local-search diverge.
title: Cohort Shuffle: Minimizing Transmission Overlap in a Contact Network
strategies: do-nothing baseline: put every person in cohort 1 (achieves B, the maximal residual risk)
sequential greedy: process people in order, assign each the cohort minimizing added residual risk against already-assigned neighbors
best-response local search: from a greedy start, repeatedly move each person to their cost-minimizing cohort until no single move improves the total
randomized multi-restart local search: run best-response from the greedy start plus several fixed-seed random starts, keep the lowest-risk assignment found
